_description: Non-obvious footguns the team has hit. One pitfall per entry. Append only; mark `status: archived` if no longer applicable.

title: stty / tput open errors leak past 2>/dev/null
description: The shell's own 'Device not configured' error on a failed `< /dev/tty` redirect is emitted before stty's `2>/dev/null` takes effect. Must wrap in a subshell: `size=$( (stty size < /dev/tty) 2>/dev/null )`. Same trick needed for `> /dev/tty` writes.

title: Do not retry the SessionStart hook approach for the banner
description: Five commits (cc1586c..c1d3f54) were reverted; see ADR a008 for the per-commit attempt log. Failure is structural, not implementation: hook is non-blocking (g009 details the /dev/tty TUI fight), hook stdout = model context (g003), no plugin lifecycle hooks exist (g010), tiered chafa portraits did not fix placement (g011). The shell wrapper works only because it runs before claude is invoked.

title: Hook stdout is captured as model context, not free UI output
description: Anything a Claude Code hook prints on stdout is added to the system prompt. ASCII portrait ~2 KB ≈ ~1500-2000 tokens per fresh session. There is no zero-token UI-only channel; stdout, hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext, stderr (styled as error), and /dev/tty are the only channels. Track upstream: anthropics/claude-code#11240.

title: PowerShell hook entry on macOS triggers a CC-visible error
description: Registering a sibling hook entry that invokes `powershell` on macOS produces `/bin/sh: powershell: command not found`. Claude Code surfaces this as a 'SessionStart:startup hook error' notice that overlaps the banner. Fix: a single sh hook that internally branches on `OS=Windows_NT` and delegates to PowerShell only when appropriate. Historical only — the current architecture is a shell wrapper, not a hook (see ADR a001 / a008).

title: BSD awk srand() has 1-second resolution
description: Back-to-back picker calls in the same wall-clock second would otherwise pick the same index. Mix PID with seconds: `seed=$(( $$ ^ $(date +%s) ))`. See bin/_pick_portrait.sh:39.

title: Chafa actual rows < requested rows for some aspect ratios
description: In side-by-side layout, computing v_offset from the requested portrait height (`ph`) pushes titles past the portrait's actual bottom; they then render at column 0 and visually break alignment. Read the actual cache_file row count via `awk 'END{print NR}' "$cache_file"` and base v_offset on that.

title: export required for config overrides — renderer runs in subprocess
description: Setting `CHAFA_SYMBOLS=...` in ~/.zshrc without `export` does nothing because banner.sh runs in a subshell of claude_with_banner. The `: "${VAR=default}"` form in banner.config.sh assigns only when unset, so an exported value (including explicit empty string `export CHAFA_FG_ONLY=""`) wins. Document this in README so users do not silently lose overrides.

title: Counts file regex gate skips manual edits with leading whitespace
description: awk's KV-line gate is `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+ [0-9]+$` (bin/_counter.sh:246). Leading spaces, tabs, or extra whitespace cause silent skip on read; write path then drops the line. Manual edits to counts.txt must use exactly one space and no leading/trailing whitespace.

title: /dev/tty writes from a background hook fight Claude Code's ink-style TUI
description: Claude Code renders with an ink-style React-to-TTY library. When a background process writes to /dev/tty after CC has drawn its TUI, CC's renderer detects the terminal-state shift and redraws — symptoms: duplicate input prompt (input box drawn twice as CC re-layouts), banner interleaved with UI (half-banner, CC repaint, more banner, then UI), banner clipped by CC's status bar / welcome header. A tiered chafa-downsampled portrait that fits the viewport did NOT fix this — the fight is structural, not size-related.

title: No plugin lifecycle hooks in Claude Code (PostInstall / OnEnable / PluginActivate)
description: A banner installed via `/plugin install` cannot confirm itself visually; the user must restart their session. There is no event the plugin can subscribe to that fires once after install. Upstream tracking: anthropics/claude-code#11240.

title: Chafa tiering fixes content sizing, not placement
description: Shipping four pre-rendered portraits (xs / sm / md / lg, 16-50 rows) and picking by `stty size` gave clean, viewport-fitting visuals — content was fine. But the SessionStart-hook race (g002) and the /dev/tty vs CC TUI fight (g009) still produced interleave artifacts on every tier. Tiering is the wrong layer to fix a placement problem.
